name: Quick Pickled Peppers
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the water, vinegar, and salt in a medium saucepan until boiling.
2. Reduce heat to simmer and cover; continue cooking until the salt is fully dissolved, approximately 3 minutes.
3. Rinse peppers thoroughly. Halve each pepper through its stem and remove stems, seeds, and white pith. Cut each half lengthwise into thirds.
4. Press garlic with the flat side of a knife and peel it.
5. Pack peppers, garlic, celery, and herb sprig into a clean 1-quart canning jar.
6. Pour hot vinegar mixture over the contents of the jar, ensuring no air bubbles remain. Use a thin rubber spatula to release any trapped air along the sides of the jar.
7. Add more vinegar mixture if necessary to cover all ingredients.
8. Seal the jar with a new canning lid and store in a cool, dark place or refrigerator for at least 1 week before consuming. The flavor improves over time.
(Note: Table salt may be used instead of kosher salt, though it could cause the brine to become cloudy.)
Nutrition Information per Piece: 8 calories, 0g protein, 2g carbohydrates, 0g fat, 101mg sodium
